summary: chain Pinned+WaitNoPop is structurally undersaturating cb_wait_front: chain only waits for 1 tile but reads tile at runtime idx=w. Original raw helpers (exp_tile_to_cb, mul_tiles_and_negative_to_cb) did cumulative cb_wait_front(cb, w+1). Pre-migration moreh_bin_chain_rt also had this pattern but tests reportedly passed — likely race that becomes visible with new T1.07/T1.08 chain stack. Fix: add explicit cb_wait_front(cb_X, w+1) before each Pinned-runtime-idx chain call in w.cpp and w_large.cpp.

fix_attempt: add cb_wait_front(cb_y, w+1) before T1.07 and T1.08 chains in w.cpp
outcome: NEUTRAL — same fail count as diagnostic baseline 24/69
note: Pre-existing pattern: moreh_bin_chain_rt callsites (already present before 7dd7cf3824a) also use Pinned+WaitNoPop with runtime idx. Either Tensix CB protocol has hidden cumulative-visibility, or the bug is elsewhere. Fix reverted to keep kernel at HEAD state.

details: Extending fp32 annotation cleanup to 5 moreh_softmax_backward kernels. Reference commit c090b2fd14c.

5 backward kernels. h.cpp/w.cpp: moreh_bin_chain, moreh_bin_chain_rt, moreh_unary_chain_rt, moreh_mul_neg_chain_rt — all need BinaryFpu/PackTile annotations. unary_chain uses CbIndexMode::Pinned (different from c090b2f forward) — keep Pinned, add CopyTileReconfig::Input. c_large/h_large/w_large: moreh_bin_chain, moreh_copy_chain, moreh_unary_chain, moreh_mul_neg_chain — same pattern as forward c090b2f.
Annotated DST_ACCUM_MODE on BinaryFpu/PackTile and CopyTileReconfig::Input on CopyTile in all 5 backward kernels (h, w, c_large, h_large, w_large). About to run logsoftmax test.

message: eltwise sweep fix: annotate fp32_dest_acc on moreh_softmax_backward kernels
note: clang-format compacted CopyTile using-decls into single lines; semantic unchanged

verdict: matches verifier baseline exactly — pre-existing forward-only failures, no regression from backward annotations
helper baseline holds (skipped count varies by env, pass count exact match)
